frozen_components: reference model for alignment supervision (frozen after explicit stage)
architecture.summary: SpiralThinker is a latent reasoning framework built on Llama-3.2-1B that performs iterative refinement of latent representations while interleaving explicit textual and implicit latent reasoning steps. The system operates in two stages: (1) explicit reasoning fine-tuning, where the model learns step-by-step textual reasoning with <bot>/<eot> boundary tokens; (2) implicit reasoning, where special <latent> tokens (delimited by <bol>/<eol>) replace alternating textual steps in training data. During inference, latent tokens are updated across multiple iterations via a lightweight adapter that projects hidden states back to embedding space, then rewritten into the sequence for subsequent forward passes. A progressive alignment objective constrains latent representations at each iteration to align with their textual counterparts extracted from a frozen reference model, stabilizing updates and maintaining coherence between implicit and explicit reasoning.

biblio.title: SpiralThinker: Latent Reasoning through an Iterative Process with Text-Latent Interleaving

claims.statement: SpiralThinker achieves 56.56% accuracy on GSM8K-Aug, surpassing the previous best (Pause Token) by 3.19 points
SpiralThinker achieves 99.40% accuracy on ProsQA, surpassing the previous best by 0.40 points
SpiralThinker achieves 63.32% accuracy on StrategyQA, exceeding the previous best by 0.44 points
On GSM8K-Aug, combining both iterative process and alignment objective yields +11.07 points over baseline with neither component
Employing the iterative process alone yields limited improvement and can even degrade performance, suggesting that iterative updates in latent representation space may drift when left unconstrained

Both iteration and alignment are essential for stable iterative reasoning; iteration provides additional reasoning depth while progressive alignment regulates multi-step evolution to keep it structured, stable, and coherent

The optimal number of latent tokens and iterations vary by dataset: best results use 4 latent tokens on GSM8K-Aug and ProsQA, 8 on StrategyQA; iterations of 4 on GSM8K-Aug, 3 on ProsQA, and 2 on StrategyQA
Softmax-based weighting schedule for progressive alignment consistently outperforms uniform weighting across iterations and achieves highest peak accuracy on GSM8K-Aug and ProsQA
